contentBlocks.original:
त्रिदलं त्रिगुणाकारं त्रिनेत्रं च त्रियायुधम् ।
त्रिजन्म पापसंहारं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tridalaṃ triguṇākāraṃ trinētraṃ cha triyāyudham ।
trijanma pāpasaṃhāraṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The three-leafed bilva, representing the three gunas, symbolizing the three-eyed Lord wielding the three weapons — offering this single bilva leaf to Shiva destroys the sins accumulated over three births.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रि-दलम् (tri-dalam) – three-leafed (tri:three | dalam:leaf); त्रि-गुण-आकारम् (tri-guṇa-ākāram) – having the form of three gunas (tri:three | guṇa:qualities | ākāram:form); त्रि-नेत्रम् (tri-nētram) – the three-eyed one (tri:three | nētram:eyes); च (cha) – and (cha:and); त्रि-आयुधम् (tri-āyudham) – wielding three weapons (tri:three | āyudham:weapons); त्रि-जन्म (tri-janma) – of three births (tri:three | janma:births); पाप-संहारम् (pāpa-saṃhāram) – destroyer of sins (pāpa:sins | saṃhāram:destruction); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिशाखैः बिल्वपत्रैश्च अच्छिद्रैः कोमलैः शुभैः ।
तवपूजां करिष्यामि एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
triśākhaiḥ bilvapatraiścha achChidraiḥ kōmalaiḥ śubhaiḥ ।
tavapūjāṃ kariṣyāmi ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With three-lobed bilva leaves that are whole, tender, and auspicious, I shall perform Your worship. This single bilva leaf is my offering to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रि-शाखैः (tri-śākhaiḥ) – with three branches/lobes (tri:three | śākhaiḥ:with branches); बिल्व-पत्रैः च (bilva-patraiḥ cha) – and with bilva leaves (bilva:bilva | patraiḥ:with leaves | cha:and); अच्छिद्रैः (achChidraiḥ) – without holes, whole (achChidraiḥ:without holes); कोमलैः (kōmalaiḥ) – tender, soft (kōmalaiḥ:tender); शुभैः (śubhaiḥ) – auspicious (śubhaiḥ:auspicious); तव पूजाम् (tava pūjām) – Your worship (tava:Your | pūjām:worship); करिष्यामि (kariṣyāmi) – I shall perform (kariṣyāmi:I shall do); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:bilva); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
कोटि कन्या महादानं तिलपर्वत कोटयः ।
काञ्चनं शैलदानेन एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kōṭi kanyā mahādānaṃ tilaparvata kōṭayaḥ ।
kāñchanaṃ śailadānēna ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The great gift of ten million maidens in marriage, or mountains of sesame seeds numbering in crores, or the donation of a golden mountain — all these are equaled by the offering of a single bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: कोटि (kōṭi) – ten million, crore (kōṭi:ten million); कन्या (kanyā) – maidens, daughters (kanyā:maidens); महादानं (mahādānaṃ) – great gift (mahā:great | dānaṃ:gift); तिल-पर्वत (tila-parvata) – mountain of sesame seeds (tila:sesame | parvata:mountain); कोटयः (kōṭayaḥ) – crores, ten millions (kōṭayaḥ:crores); काञ्चनं (kāñchanaṃ) – golden (kāñchanaṃ:golden); शैल-दानेन (śaila-dānēna) – by the gift of a mountain (śaila:mountain | dānēna:by gift); एक-बिल्वं (ēka-bilvaṃ) – a single bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvaṃ:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
काशीक्षेत्र निवासं च कालभैरव दर्शनम् ।
प्रयागे माधवं दृष्ट्वा एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāśīkṣētra nivāsaṃ cha kālabhairava darśanam ।
prayāgē mādhavaṃ dṛṣṭvā ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Residing in the sacred field of Kashi and beholding Kalabhairava, having seen Madhava at Prayaga — offering a single bilva leaf to Shiva equals all these merits.
contentBlocks.meanings: काशी-क्षेत्र (kāśī-kṣētra) – the sacred field of Kashi (kāśī:Kashi | kṣētra:field/sacred place); निवासम् (nivāsam) – residence, dwelling (nivāsam:residence); च (cha) – and (cha:and); काल-भैरव (kāla-bhairava) – Kalabhairava, the fierce form of time (kāla:time | bhairava:the fierce one); दर्शनम् (darśanam) – vision, beholding (darśanam:seeing/vision); प्रयागे (prayāgē) – at Prayaga (prayāgē:at Prayaga); माधवम् (mādhavam) – Madhava, Lord Vishnu (mādhavam:Madhava); दृष्ट्वा (dṛṣṭvā) – having seen (dṛṣṭvā:having seen); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – a single bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
इन्दुवारे व्रतं स्थित्वा निराहारो महेश्वराः ।
नक्तं हौष्यामि देवेश एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
induvārē vrataṃ sthitvā nirāhārō mahēśvarāḥ ।
naktaṃ hauṣyāmi dēvēśa ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Great Lord Maheshvara, having observed the vow on Monday while remaining without food, at night I shall offer this single bilva leaf as an offering to Shiva, O Lord of the Gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: इन्दु-वारे (indu-vārē) – on Monday (indu:moon | vārē:on the day); व्रतं (vrataṃ) – vow, observance (vrataṃ:vow); स्थित्वा (sthitvā) – having remained, having observed (sthitvā:having stayed); निराहारः (nirāhāraḥ) – without food, fasting (nir:without | āhāraḥ:food); महेश्वराः (mahēśvarāḥ) – O Great Lord (mahā:great | īśvarāḥ:lord); नक्तम् (naktaṃ) – at night (naktaṃ:at night); हौष्यामि (hauṣyāmi) – I shall offer (hauṣyāmi:I shall offer); देवेश (dēvēśa) – O Lord of the Gods (dēva:gods | īśa:lord); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvaṃ) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvaṃ:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
रामलिङ्ग प्रतिष्ठा च वैवाहिक कृतं तथा ।
तटाकानिच सन्तानं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaliṅga pratiṣṭhā cha vaivāhika kṛtaṃ tathā ।
taṭākānicha santānaṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The consecration of a Shiva Linga, the sacred rite of marriage performed with devotion, the construction of tanks and reservoirs, and the blessing of progeny — all these merits are attained by offering a single Bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: रामलिङ्ग (rāmaliṅga) – Shiva Linga (rāma:beautiful | liṅga:sacred symbol of Shiva); प्रतिष्ठा (pratiṣṭhā) – consecration, installation (pratiṣṭhā:consecration); च (cha) – and (cha:and); वैवाहिक (vaivāhika) – pertaining to marriage (vaivāhika:matrimonial); कृतं (kṛtaṃ) – done, performed (kṛtaṃ:performed); तथा (tathā) – also, likewise (tathā:likewise); तटाकानि (taṭākāni) – tanks, reservoirs (taṭākāni:water tanks); च (cha) – and (cha:and); सन्तानं (santānaṃ) – progeny, offspring (santānaṃ:progeny); एक-बिल्वं (ēka-bilvaṃ) – one Bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvaṃ:Bilva leaf); शिवार्पणम् (śivārpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
अखण्ड बिल्वपत्रं च आयुतं शिवपूजनम् ।
कृतं नाम सहस्रेण एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
akhaṇḍa bilvapatraṃ cha āyutaṃ śivapūjanam ।
kṛtaṃ nāma sahasrēṇa ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: An unbroken bilva leaf offered in worship of Shiva equals ten thousand acts of devotion. The merit earned by chanting a thousand divine names is attained by offering a single bilva leaf to Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: अखण्ड (akhaṇḍa) – unbroken, whole (akhaṇḍa:unbroken); बिल्व-पत्रम् (bilva-patram) – bilva leaf (bilva:bilva tree | patram:leaf); च (cha) – and (cha:and); आयुतम् (āyutam) – ten thousand (āyutam:ten thousand); शिव-पूजनम् (śiva-pūjanam) – worship of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | pūjanam:worship); कृतम् (kṛtam) – done, performed (kṛtam:done); नाम (nāma) – name (nāma:name); सहस्रेण (sahasrēṇa) – by a thousand (sahasrēṇa:by a thousand); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:bilva); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
उमया सहदेवेश नन्दि वाहनमेव च ।
भस्मलेपन सर्वाङ्गं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
umayā sahadēvēśa nandi vāhanamēva cha ।
bhasmalēpana sarvāṅgaṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord of Gods together with Uma, with Nandi as His vehicle, His entire body smeared with sacred ash — I offer one bilva leaf to Shiva as a sacred offering.
contentBlocks.meanings: उमया (umayā) – with Uma (umayā:with Uma); सह-देवेश (saha-dēvēśa) – together with the Lord of Gods (saha:together with | dēvēśa:Lord of Gods); नन्दि-वाहनम् (nandi-vāhanam) – having Nandi as vehicle (nandi:Nandi | vāhanam:vehicle); एव च (ēva cha) – indeed and (ēva:indeed | cha:and); भस्म-लेपन (bhasma-lēpana) – smeared with ash (bhasma:sacred ash | lēpana:smearing); सर्वाङ्गम् (sarvāṅgam) – entire body (sarva:all | aṅgam:body); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvaṃ) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvaṃ:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
सालग्रामेषु विप्राणां तटाकं दशकूपयोः ।
यज्ञकोटि सहस्रस्य एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sālagrāmēṣu viprāṇāṃ taṭākaṃ daśakūpayōḥ ।
yajñakōṭi sahasrasya ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The merit of offering Shalagrama stones to Brahmins, the merit of constructing a tank (pond) equal to ten wells, the merit of a thousand crores of sacrifices — all these are equaled by offering a single Bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: सालग्रामेषु (sālagrāmēṣu) – in Shalagrama stones (sālagrāmēṣu:in Shalagrama stones); विप्राणाम् (viprāṇām) – of/to Brahmins (viprāṇām:of Brahmins); तटाकम् (taṭākam) – a tank, reservoir (taṭākam:tank); दश-कूपयोः (daśa-kūpayōḥ) – of ten wells (daśa:ten | kūpayōḥ:of wells); यज्ञ-कोटि-सहस्रस्य (yajña-kōṭi-sahasrasya) – of a thousand crores of sacrifices (yajña:sacrifice | kōṭi:crore | sahasrasya:of a thousand); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – a single Bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:Bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
दन्ति कोटि सहस्रेषु अश्वमेधशतक्रतौ ।
कोटिकन्या महादानं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
danti kōṭi sahasrēṣu aśvamēdhaśatakratau ।
kōṭikanyā mahādānaṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Superior to the gift of thousands of crores of elephants, superior to a hundred Ashvamedha sacrifices, superior even to the great donation of a crore of maidens in marriage — is the offering of a single Bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: दन्ति-कोटि-सहस्रेषु (danti-kōṭi-sahasrēṣu) – among thousands of crores of elephants (danti:elephant | kōṭi:crore | sahasrēṣu:in thousands); अश्वमेध-शत-क्रतौ (aśvamēdha-śata-kratau) – in a hundred Ashvamedha sacrifices (aśvamēdha:horse sacrifice | śata:hundred | kratau:in sacrifices); कोटि-कन्या (kōṭi-kanyā) – a crore of maidens (kōṭi:crore | kanyā:maidens); महादानम् (mahādānam) – the great gift (mahā:great | dānam:gift); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – a single Bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:Bilva leaf); शिवार्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
बिल्वाणां दर्शनं पुण्यं स्पर्शनं पापनाशनम् ।
अघोर पापसंहारं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bilvāṇāṃ darśanaṃ puṇyaṃ sparśanaṃ pāpanāśanam ।
aghōra pāpasaṃhāraṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The mere sight of Bilva leaves brings merit, and their touch destroys sin. The fierce destroyer of all sins — I offer one Bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: बिल्वाणाम् (bilvāṇām) – of Bilva leaves (bilvāṇām:of Bilva leaves); दर्शनम् (darśanam) – sight, seeing (darśanam:sight); पुण्यम् (puṇyam) – meritorious, sacred (puṇyam:merit); स्पर्शनम् (sparśanam) – touch, touching (sparśanam:touch); पाप-नाशनम् (pāpa-nāśanam) – destroyer of sin (pāpa:sin | nāśanam:destroyer); अघोर (aghōra) – the fierce one, Shiva in his terrible aspect (aghōra:fierce, terrible); पाप-संहारम् (pāpa-saṃhāram) – annihilation of sins (pāpa:sin | saṃhāram:annihilation); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – one Bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:Bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
सहस्रवेद पाठेषु ब्रह्मस्तापनमुच्यते ।
अनेकव्रत कोटीनां एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sahasravēda pāṭhēṣu brahmastāpanamuchyatē ।
anēkavrata kōṭīnāṃ ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The recitation of the Vedas a thousand times is said to equal the Brahma Tapana ritual. The offering of a single Bilva leaf to Lord Shiva is equivalent to performing millions of sacred vows.
contentBlocks.meanings: सहस्र-वेद (sahasra-vēda) – thousand Vedas (sahasra:thousand | vēda:Vedas); पाठेषु (pāṭhēṣu) – in recitations (pāṭhēṣu:in recitations); ब्रह्म-स्तापनम् (brahma-stāpanam) – the Brahma Tapana ritual (brahma:Brahma | stāpanam:heating/austerity ritual); उच्यते (uchyatē) – is said (uchyatē:is said); अनेक-व्रत (anēka-vrata) – many vows (anēka:many | vrata:vows); कोटीनाम् (kōṭīnām) – of millions (kōṭīnām:of millions); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvam) – one Bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:Bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
अन्नदान सहस्रेषु सहस्रोपनयनं तथा ।
अनेक जन्मपापानि एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
annadāna sahasrēṣu sahasrōpanayanaṃ tathā ।
anēka janmapāpāni ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: A thousand charitable offerings of food, a thousand sacred thread ceremonies — all the sins accumulated over countless births are absolved by offering a single bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: अन्न-दान (anna-dāna) – gift of food (anna:food | dāna:gift/charity); सहस्रेषु (sahasrēṣu) – in thousands (sahasrēṣu:in thousands); सहस्र-उपनयनम् (sahasra-upanayanaṃ) – a thousand sacred thread ceremonies (sahasra:thousand | upanayanaṃ:sacred thread initiation); तथा (tathā) – likewise, and also (tathā:likewise); अनेक (anēka) – many, countless (anēka:many); जन्म-पापानि (janma-pāpāni) – sins of births (janma:birth | pāpāni:sins); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvaṃ) – a single bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvaṃ:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
बिल्वाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेच्छिव सन्निधौ ।
शिवलोकमवाप्नोति एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bilvāṣṭakamidaṃ puṇyaṃ yaḥ paṭhēcchiva sannidhau ।
śivalōkamavāpnōti ēkabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites this sacred Bilvashtakam in the presence of Lord Shiva attains Shivaloka, the divine abode of Shiva. Even a single bilva leaf offered to Shiva becomes a complete act of devotion.
contentBlocks.meanings: बिल्व-अष्टकम् (bilva-aṣṭakam) – the eight verses on bilva (bilva:bilva leaf | aṣṭakam:set of eight); इदम् पुण्यम् (idaṃ puṇyaṃ) – this meritorious/sacred (idam:this | puṇyam:meritorious); यः पठेत् (yaḥ paṭhēt) – whoever recites (yaḥ:whoever | paṭhēt:recites); शिव-सन्निधौ (śiva-sannidhau) – in the presence of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | sannidhau:in the presence of); शिव-लोकम् (śiva-lōkam) – the abode of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | lōkam:world/abode); अवाप्नोति (avāpnōti) – attains (avāpnōti:attains); एक-बिल्वम् (ēka-bilvaṃ) – one bilva leaf (ēka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);
